---
title: "Homework 5"
format: html
---
###### Homework 5 ######
## Sofia Puccio 02/11/2026 ###

##Question 1## 
```{r}
# Create a funtion that takes the parameters required to run a logistic growth model and returns a dataframe with columns for population size and time.
logistic_growth <- function(N0, r, K, tfinal, tstep) {
  
  # Create time vector
  time <- seq(from = 0, to = tfinal, by = tstep)
  
  # Closed-form logistic growth equation
  N <- K / (1 + ((K - N0) / N0) * exp(-r * time))
  
  # Return data frame
  output <- data.frame(
    time = time,
    population = N
  )
  
  return(output)
}
# Inputting values to make sure that this code works 
# This will ideally return a data frame with time going from 0 to tfinal and a population at each time mark 
log_df <- logistic_growth(
  N0 = 10,
  r = 0.3,
  K = 100,
  tfinal = 50,
  tstep = 0.1
)

head(log_df)
#sucsess! 

# Question 2###
# Create a second function that takes the above dataframe as its input and creates a plot of population size over time as a side effect.
# Taking a dataframe that is produced by the population model and creating a plot of population size over time 

# load the necessary package from library 
library(ggplot2)
# create the plot 
plot_population <- function(df) {
  
  plot(
    x = df$time,
    y = df$N,
    type = "l",
    lwd = 2,
    xlab = "Time",
    ylab = "Population Size",
    main = "Population Growth Over Time"
  )
}
# "2 lines of code requirement"
df <- logistic_growth(N0 = 10, r = 0.02, K = 100, tfinal = 500, tstep = 1)
plot_population(df)

```
